====={{aniseries|GS}}=====
[[File:Clair anime.png|thumb|left|220px|Clair in ''Pokémon the Series: Gold and Silver'']]
Clair hosted {{Ash}} and {{ashfr}} when they stayed in [[Blackthorn City]] from ''[[EP251|Beauty is Skin Deep]]'' to the beginning of ''[[EP255|Why? Wynaut!]]''. As Ash and his friends neared Blackthorn City, they discovered a {{p|Dratini}} and a {{p|Gyarados}} in a river. They discovered that both Pokémon belonged to Clair, who was following Dratini down the river as it prepared to shed its skin. {{TRT}} snatched Dratini, however, and Clair gave chase. Dratini escaped from Team Rocket, and Ash fought to protect it. During the ensuing {{pkmn|battle}}, an electric shock was headed straight for Dratini, but Clair jumped in front of it to absorb the shock. Dratini witnessed its {{pkmn|Trainer}}'s devotion, and [[Evolution|evolved]] into a {{TP|Clair|Dragonair}} in order to put a stop to Team Rocket. Later, Clair was finally able to perform an important ritual to purify the legendary [[Dragon Fang]] at Riverhead Falls, with her Gym assistant [[Kaburagi]] and Ash and his friends in attendance. Afterwards, Clair went on to accept Ash's challenge for a {{Badge|Rising}}.

In ''[[EP252|Fangs for Nothin']]'', Ash and Clair began their Gym battle. Clair quickly gained the upper hand, but the battle was cut short when Team Rocket stole the Dragon Fang from the Gym's treasure room and set off the security alarms in the process. Ash, {{ashfr}}, Clair, and Kaburagi chased after Team Rocket and they all ended up in the [[Dragon Holy Land]], where Team Rocket tricked a {{p|Dragonite}} into helping them. In the [[EP253|following episode]], {{jo|Liza}} and [[Ash's Charizard]] arrived on the scene, whereupon they helped calm down the raging Dragonite and put out the raging fire that threatened the Dragon Holy Land.

Clair and Ash had their [[rematch]] in ''[[EP254|Better Eight Than Never]]''. Clair called on her {{p|Kingdra}}, but it was left exhausted by its confrontation against [[Ash's Snorlax]] and was knocked out by an {{m|Ice Punch}}. In the next round, Clair's Gyarados defeated Snorlax by {{status|paralyze|paralzying}} it with a {{m|Dragon Breath}} attack. Later, {{AP|Pikachu}}'s speed and supereffective {{m|Thunderbolt}} attacks allowed him to defeat Gyarados. Clair's Dragonair entered the battle and took out Pikachu, then faced off against Charizard. The round was initially in Clair's favor until Charizard managed to grab a hold of Dragonair and defeat it with a "Flaming {{m|Seismic Toss}}" combination, thus earning Ash his final [[Johto League]] Badge.

In ''[[EP255|Why? Wynaut!]]'', Clair informed Ash that the [[Silver Conference]] would take place in {{jo|Silver Town}}, located at the foot of [[Mt. Silver]], in three months.
